name: Wreath Bread
recipeInstructions:
------------------------FLOUR WILL BE USED IN 4 PART------------------------
2 1/2 cups Whole wheat pastry flour (1)
3 1/4 cups Whole wheat pastry flour (3)
2 cups Whole wheat pastry flour (2)
3/4 cup Whole wheat pastry flour (4)
1. In a large bowl, combine maple syrup, yeast, and 2 1/2 cups of the whole wheat pastry flour.
2. In a saucepan, heat apple juice and butter until almost melted.
3. Gradually beat the liquid into the yeast mixture with an electric mixer.
4. Add egg yolks, remaining eggs, lemon peel, almond extract, and 2 cups of the flour to make a thick batter.
5. Stir in 3/4 cup of the flour to form dough. Turn onto a floured work surface and knead until smooth, adding more flour as needed (at least 15 minutes).
6. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, turning to coat the top. Cover and let rise until doubled, about 1 hour.
7. Punch down the dough and turn out onto a floured surface. Divide into 3 equal parts for each loaf.
8. Cover and let rest for 15 minutes.
9. Lightly oil two baking sheets.
10. On a floured work surface, roll each part into a 24-inch length. Place three lengths together and braid; it works best to start in the middle and move toward the ends.
11. Shape into a ring, joining the ends and pinching together. Repeat with remaining loaves.
12. Cover rings with towels and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
13. Beat egg whites and brush over the loaves.
14. Top each ring with sliced almonds.
15. Place in an oven preheated to 350°F and bake until golden brown and produces a thunking sound when tapped, about 15-20 minutes total.
